title: Parcel storage system that reduces movements and operations performed by an entity for discharging and retrieval of parcels
abstract: The present invention relates to a space-saving parcel storage unit (100) designed to enhance storage efficiency and improve ergonomic handling of parcels. This storage unit incorporates one or more storage containers (108A-N), one or more flaps (110A-N) arranged in a predetermined configuration, and a lever unit (112) that controls the movement of the one or more flaps (110A-N). The parcel storage unit effectively reduces the need for at least one entity (102), whether human operators or automated machines, to reach into hard-to-access areas within the parcel storage unit that may pose challenges due to depth, height, or obstruction by other components.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS I/Wc Claim: 1. A space-saving parcel storage unit (100) for storing parcels with a reduced number of operations or movements by an entity, comprising: one or more storage containers (108A-N) for storing parcels; one or more flaps (110A-N) positioned in a predetermined configuration relative to the one or more storage containers (108A-N); and a lever unit (112) mechanically attached to the one or more flaps (110A-N), wherein the lever unit ( 112) is configured to control the angular position of the one or more flaps ( 110A- N), wherein, when the parcels are to be stored in a first desired storage container, the parcel storage unit (100) enables at least one entity (102) to (i) actuate the lever unit (112) to move the one or more flaps (110A-N) to a desired position, and (ii) discharge parcels onto the first desired storage container after moving the one or more flaps (110A-N) to the desired position; and wherein, when the parcels are to be stored in a second desired storage container, the parcel storage unit (100) enables at least one entity (102) to (i) discharge parcels onto the one or more flaps (110A-N), and (ii) actuate the lever unit (112) to move the one or more flaps (110A-N) to a position that allows parcels to transfer from the one or more flaps (110A-N) to the second desired storage container. 2. The parcel storage unit (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the lever unit (112) is actuated by the at least one entity (102) through the application of mechanical force, allowing the lever unit (112) to transfer this force to actuate the one or more flaps (110A-N). 3. The parcel storage unit (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the at least one entity (102) includes multiple activation points to initiate actions by analyzing instructions for discharging parcels into their respective storage containers through the actuation of the one or more flaps 

(110A-N). 4. The parcel storage unit (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the one or more flaps (110A-N) are mechanically linked through a motion-transmitting mechanism for simultaneous actuation with the lever unit (112). 5. A method for storing parcels using a space-saving parcel storage unit (100) while reducing physical footprint and improving ergonomics, comprising: providing a parcel storage unit (100) that includes: (i) a rack arrangement with one or more storage containers (108A-N) for storing parcels; (ii) one or more flaps (110A-N) positioned in a predetermined configuration relative to the storage containers (108A-N); and (iii) a lever unit (112) mechanically attached to the one or more flaps (110A-N), with the lever unit (112) configured to control the angular position of the one or more flaps (110A-N); wherein, when the parcels are to be stored in a first desired storage container, the method comprises: enabling at least one entity (102) to (i) actuate the lever unit (112) to move the one or more flaps (110A-N) to a desired position, and (ii) discharge parcels onto the first desired storage container after the one or more flaps (110A-N) have been moved to the desired position; and wherein, when the parcels are to be stored in a second desired storage container, the method comprises: enabling at least one entity (102) to (i) discharge parcels onto the one or more flaps (110A-N), and (ii) actuate the lever unit (112) to move the one or more flaps (110A-N) to a position that permits parcels to move from the one or more flaps ( 110A-N) to the second desired storage container.
